title: User Management
content:
User story description ---> 

As a  Portal Administrator,

I want  see a full list of all users in my organisation on the Admin → User Management tab — showing each user's avatar, name, email, role, status, last active time, and account access state — and be able to search by name or email in real time

So that  I can quickly assess the health of my portal user base, identify inactive or access-revoked users, verify role assignments, and find any specific user without exporting data or raising a support request.

Flow:

  1. User navigates to base url defined in env varibale

  2. clicks on salesforce login button

  3. lands on admin page>> active tab should be : User Management

Acceptance criteria --->

The Admin page renders three tabs: "User Management", "Roles & Access", "Audit Log". User Management is the default active tab on page load.

The User Management tab header shows "N Users" (total count), a "Search user" input field, and an "Invite user" teal button. All three are visible simultaneously.

The user table renders five columns in this exact order: User information, Role, Status, Last active, Account Access. No other columns are present.

The User information cell renders: (a) a circular avatar — photo if available from SSO/identity provider, or initials monogram (first letter of first name + first letter of last name) with a deterministic colour otherwise, (b) full name in bold on the first line, (c) email address in muted text below the name.

Since the invite is only on the basis of e-mail id, it may be largely possible that we have only email ids in user, in that case the text should appear as per the mock app design

The Role cell shows the plain text role label for that user (e.g. "Admin", "Finance", "Business"). No badge styling on the role label.

The Status cell shows a green "Active" badge for active users and a red "Inactive" badge for inactive users.

The Last active cell shows a human-readable relative time string (e.g. "19 days ago"). If the user has never logged in, the cell shows "Never".

The Account Access cell contains a toggle switch. Active users with access enabled show a teal/ON toggle. Users with access revoked (whether active or inactive) show a grey/OFF toggle. There is a direct visual correlation: Inactive users always have their toggle OFF.

The Search user input filters the table in real time as the user types. Filtering is case-insensitive and matches on name or email. A minimum of 1 character triggers filtering.

If the search query matches no users, the table body shows: "No users found for '[query]'." No broken layout.

The currently logged-in Portal Administrator's row is visible in the table but their Account Access toggle is permanently disabled (non-interactive). A tooltip on hover reads: "You cannot revoke your own access."

The table is scrollable when the user count exceeds the viewport. Pagination is applied at 25 rows; page controls render below the table for accounts with more than 25 users.

create only 1 e2e test case which covers all acceptance criteria
